text:
When Oliver turned seventeen, he fell into an obscure bird watching society. Every week at dusk he went out into the woods. One night he came face-to-face with an owl. His heart just skipped. That evening he took pictures. That night he slept. Owls sleep a lot.

That autumn his photos came alive. Some owlets wanted company. One evening there were five on his windowsill. Every afternoon the girls visited. Sometimes Oliver liked that more than the quiet woods. But the next year Oliver went away. The owlets didn't come.

Now fifty years old Oliver visits owl pellets. That's his passion. He's proud when his little picture is in the center.

One day he met Ruby. She loved stars. She would always say owls.

"That could be me someday," Oliver said.




I love owls, Ruby said.
That's you sometimes; just take your time.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
